description: NIST CFReDS 'Hacking Case' (Greg Schardt / 'Mr. Evil') — Windows XP image of a machine used for wardriving and credential theft: NetStumbler, Ethereal, WinPcap, Cain & Abel, Look@LAN, and password-recovery tools (e.g. 123WASP) were installed and run.
notes: Split raw image (.001-.00N). XP-era logs are .evt (not .evtx) so EVTX coverage is limited; relies on prefetch/amcache/registry.

expected_findings.note: Hacking/sniffing/credential tools installed and executed. Engine surfaces 123WASP_SETUP.EXE via ghost-binary (R5) — matches on 'wasp'.
Tool present in execution artifacts but removed from disk.
